name: Cal.com API
what_it_does: Open-source scheduling platform API for managing event types, bookings, availability, and calendar integrations with the ability to self-host.
use_cases: Creating and managing booking event types from agents
Full booking lifecycle management (create, reschedule, cancel)
Availability management and time slot queries
Multi-calendar integration for complex scheduling scenarios
not_for: Teams needing enterprise scheduling features without technical setup
Simple one-off meeting links (Calendly is simpler)
best_when: A developer-focused agent needs full control over scheduling with either the Cal.com cloud API or a self-hosted deployment.
avoid_when: You need a no-code scheduling tool or are not comfortable with API integration.

auth.notes: API keys from Cal.com settings. OAuth2 for platform integrations. Self-hosted instances use their own auth. V2 API uses Bearer token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free cloud plan with unlimited bookings and event types
pricing.notes: Fully functional free tier. Can self-host for free. API access on all tiers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: V2 API has improved error messages over V1. OpenAPI spec is available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Bookings have unique UIDs. Creating same booking slot twice may fail with conflict.
known_agent_gotchas: V1 and V2 APIs coexist with different patterns - use V2 for new agents
Self-hosted deployments require managing your own API URL base
Time zone handling requires IANA timezone names
